prerequisites: A running ClickHouse server (self-managed or ClickHouse Cloud) reachable from the connector.
The hostname or IP of the ClickHouse server (the host config field) and the port it listens on (the port config field).
The name of the database to read from (the database config field).
A ClickHouse user account (the username config field) with permission to read the target database and tables.
The password for that user (the password config field), unless the account is configured with an empty password.
If connecting over an SSH bastion, network access to the SSH host plus either an SSH private key or the SSH user's password (the tunnel_method fields).

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a ClickHouse user and password. Create the user in ClickHouse with SQL-driven access control, for example CREATE USER clickhouse_admin IDENTIFIED BY 'password', then supply that user in the username field and its password in the password field. If no password is set on the account, the password may be left empty.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (key-based)
authMethods.summary: Optional. Reach a ClickHouse server that is not directly exposed by tunneling through an SSH bastion host using an SSH private key. Configure tunnel_method with the SSH host and user, and provide the private key in tunnel_method.ssh_key. ClickHouse user and password are still required for the database itself.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (password-based)
authMethods.summary: Optional. Reach a ClickHouse server through an SSH bastion host using the SSH user's password. Configure tunnel_method and provide the SSH password in tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password. ClickHouse user and password are still required for the database itself.

setupSteps.title: Create a ClickHouse user for the connector
setupSteps.body: On the ClickHouse server, enable SQL-driven access control and create a dedicated user with a password, for example: CREATE USER clickhouse_admin IDENTIFIED BY 'password'. Grant it read access to the database and tables you want to sync. ClickHouse's default user account is used when no username is supplied, but a dedicated least-privilege user is recommended.

setupSteps.title: Confirm host, port, and database
setupSteps.body: Determine the ClickHouse server hostname or IP (host) and the port it listens on (port). Identify the database name (database) you want to extract from.

setupSteps.title: Enter connection details
setupSteps.body: Set host, port, database, and username from the values above, and enter the account's password in the password field. Leave password empty only if the ClickHouse account is configured with no password.

setupSteps.title: Enable SSL if your server uses TLS
setupSteps.body: If the ClickHouse server is configured for encrypted connections, turn on the ssl option so the connector negotiates a TLS connection. Leave it off for plaintext connections.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel if needed (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the database is only reachable through a bastion host, configure tunnel_method with the SSH host and user. Provide either an SSH private key in tunnel_method.ssh_key or the SSH user's password in tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password.

setupSteps.title: Add JDBC URL parameters if needed (optional)
setupSteps.body: Use the jdbc_url_params field to pass extra connection parameters to the underlying JDBC driver as key=value pairs when you need to tune the connection beyond the standard options.

sources.title: ClickHouse access control and account management
